rulings.Case Title: CHARLES E. YEAGER et al VS. WILD, CARTER & TIPTON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Against Plaintiffs Based On The Underlying Hoa Action And Motion For Summary Judgment Against Plaintiff Victoria Yeager
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, January 20, 2015, Line 16, DEFENDANT DE LA PENA & MCDONALD, LLP, CHARLES MURRAY, MCDONALD, STEVEN E. (SUED AS STEPHEN E. MCDONALD) MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Against Plaintiffs Based On The Underlying Hoa Action And Motion For Summary Judgment Against Plaintiff Victoria Yeager. Motion for Summary Adjudication as to the Fifth Cause of Action for Professional Negligence in the HOA matter is granted. Defendants meet their initial burden by presenting evidence that Defendants withdrew as counsel three years before the trial in the case began, Plaintiffs were subsequently represented by new counsel, Plaintiffs had judgment entered against them because the Court found they were unjustly enriched and not because of any identifiable mistake of Defendants, and Plaintiffs' trial counsel was responsible for presenting evidence in support of the cross-complaint. (Defendants' Undisputed Material Facts ("UMF") 22, 25, 27, 34-35, 38-43, 46-48, 55.) Plaintiffs' evidence does not raise a triable issue of material fact as to whether Plaintiffs would have achieved a better result at trial, but for Defendants' conduct. The Declaration of Victoria Yeager speculates that had Defendants done certain things differently, the trial court would have come to a different conclusion. "Responsive evidence that gives rise to no more than mere speculation is not sufficient to establish a triable issue of material fact." (Carlsen v. Koivumaki (2014) 227 Cal.App.4th 879, 889-890.) Motion for Summary Judgment as to Plaintiff Victoria Yeager is denied. Defendants meet their initial burden by providing evidence showing that Defendants only represented Plaintiffs in five cases. (Declaration of Steven McDonald, par. 4.) The Declaration of Victoria Yeager raises a triable issue of material fact as to whether Defendants represented Plaintiffs in more than five matters. (Declaration of Victoria Yeager, par. 65.) The First Amended Complaint is broadly written and does not limit the cause of action to only the five cases identified by Defendants. (First Amended Complaint, par. 28.) The evidentiary objections will be addressed in a separate order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: SUZANNE AGASI VS. MICHELLE TON-NU WAYLAND et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Assignment Order And For Order Restraining Judgment Debtor
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, January 20, 2015, Line 5, PLAINTIFF SUZANNE AGASI Motion For Assignment Order And For Order Restraining Judgment Debtor. Motion denied. The Court has broad discretion in determining whether to order an assignment, and may consider all relevant factors, including the reasonable economic needs of a natural person judgment debtor. (CCP § 708.510(c).) Based on the evidence submitted with the Judgment Creditor's declaration, all of the rent collectable by the Judgment Creditor is necessary for Creditor to meet her reasonable economic needs. rulings.Case Title: JOSHUA WILLIAMS et al VS. SWISSPORT NORTH AMERICA, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORAT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, January 20, 2015, Line 6, PLAINTIFF JOSHUA WILLIAMS, ROGER VALLE Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint. Granted. Plaintiffs may file the proposed first amended complaint within 10 days of entry of this order. "There is a policy of great liberality in permitting amendments to the pleadings at any stage of the proceeding." (Berman v. Bromberg (1997) 56 Cal.App.4th 936, 945.) However, the trial court has discretion to deny a motion for leave to amend if the plaintiff was dilatory and the defendant will be prejudiced by the amendment. (Hirsa v. Sup. Ct. (1981) 118 Cal.App.3d 486, 490.) While Defendant has established that Plaintiffs were dilatory in seeking amendment as to the Labor Code wage and hour claims, Defendant has not established that they will be unduly prejudiced by the amendment. The trial date is not until October 2015, giving Defendant time to conduct additional discovery, if necessary. Further, wage and hour violations were pled in the original complaint in the PAGA and Violation of Bus. and Prof. Code sec. 17200 causes of action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: WATCHANAPORN CHANGTO AND SAKSEE ISANPAYU VS. 2001 THAI STICK, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Written Discovery And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 20, 2015, Line 1, PLAINTIFFS WATCHANAPORN CHANGTO, SAKSEE ISANPAYU's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Written Discovery And Request For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: HEARING REQUIRED. THE TENTATIVE RULING IS TO GRANT TO MOTION TO COMPEL AND AWARD SANCTIONS. TO BE ADDRESSED IS WHETHER THE ORDER OF THIS COURT DATED FEBRUARY 25, 2014 HAS A RELTIONSHIP WITH THE DISCOVERY SOUGHT HEREIN. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: THE HOME DEPOT AND HELMSMAN MANAGEMENT SERVICES VS. RAYMOND JETER, AN INDIVIDUAL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, January 20, 2015, Line 10, CROSS DEFENDANT THE HOME DEPOT AND HELMSMAN MANAGEMENT SERVICES DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT. The demurrer to the breach of contract cause of action is overruled. Plaintiff does not show that Defendant's construction of the settlement agreement is clearly erroneous. A demurrer to a cause of action for breach of contract admits not only the contents of the contract, but also any pleaded meaning that the instrument is reasonably susceptible. See Aragon-Haas v. Family Security Ins. Services, Inc., (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 232, 239 ("While plaintiffs' interpretation of the contract may prove invalid, it [is] improper to resolve the issue against her solely on her own pleading."). The demurrer to the fraud cause of action is overruled. Plaintiff alleges sufficient facts to support the claim and less particularity is required when the defendant should possess full information regarding the dispute. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: KAZIM Z. ACAR VS. NEW YORK LIFE
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, January 20, 2015, Line 14, DEFENDANT NEW YORK LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. The Complaint indicates that Defendant would re-hire Plaintiff if Plaintiff repaid the alleged debt. (Complaint pgs. 2:22-23; 3:3-4; 4:19-20). The allegations of the complaint show that Plaintiff has not repaid the debt. (Complaint pg. 5:5-16). Because Plaintiff has not performed the condition or alleged that he is in the process of performing and Defendant wrongfully repudiated the contract, Plaintiff cannot allege the cause of action. Plaintiff has not demonstrated how he can cure the defect. rulings.Case Title: JIN YAN ZHU ET AL VS. JESSICA H LEE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, January 20, 2015, Line 15, DEFENDANT JESSICA LEE DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer overruled in part and sustained in part. The Demurrer is overruled on the grounds of failure to exhaust arbitration remedies and lack of subject matter jurisdiction without prejudice to the filing of a motion to compel arbitration. The Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to the cause of action for breach of contract in order to clarify whether the allegedly breached contract is oral or written, and the parties to the agreement. The Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to the cause of action for breach of securities laws in order for Plaintiff to clarify the grounds and authority for a private cause of action for violations of law.
